---
description: "File naming, folder responsibilities, game data formats, asset rules, and constants conventions for the renderer"
applyTo: "src/game/**/*.{ts,tsx,json,css}"
---

# File & Data Conventions

When adding new files, data definitions, or assets to the renderer, follow the standards in [docs/FILE_CONVENTIONS.md](../../docs/FILE_CONVENTIONS.md). That file is the authoritative source — this summary provides quick-reference rules for the most common decisions. When in doubt, consult the full doc. For the complete set of project conventions, see all documents in the `docs/` folder.

---

## Folder Responsibilities

- `engine/` — Game logic. No React, no DOM. Never imports from `ui/`.
- `data/` — Static game data. Typed `.ts` exports only. No side effects, no imports from `engine/` or `ui/`.
- `ui/components/` — Shared React components (`GameButton`, `Modal`, `Tooltip`…).
- `ui/screens/` — Full screens (`MainMenu`, `Settings`, `GameOver`…).
- `ui/overlays/` — In-game HUD layers (`HealthBar`, `Minimap`…).
- `styles/` — CSS files. All custom properties in `variables.css`.
- `assets/` — Runtime-loaded files only (sprites, audio, fonts, maps).
- `utils/` — Pure helper functions. No React hooks, no DOM, no engine refs.

## Naming

- **Folders:** `camelCase` — `engine`, `entities`, `components`.
- **Class/component files:** `PascalCase` — `Player.ts`, `GameButton.tsx`.
- **Function/data/constant files:** `camelCase` — `math.ts`, `enemies.ts`, `constants.ts`.
- **Asset files:** `kebab-case` — `goblin-idle.png`, `sword-swing.ogg`. No spaces.
- **CSS files:** `camelCase` — `variables.css`, `gameButton.css`.

---

## Game Data

### Default format: TypeScript

Static definitions (enemies, skills, items, loot tables) are **typed `.ts` files** in `src/game/data/`.

- Define all interfaces in `data/types.ts` — single source of truth for data shapes.
- One file per category: `enemies.ts`, `skills.ts`, `items.ts`.
- Export `readonly` arrays typed against the interface. Use `as const` where possible.
- IDs are `string`, unique, `kebab-case`: `'fire-elemental'`, not `'FireElemental'` or `3`.
- No logic in data files — no functions, no computed values, no conditionals.
- Keep entries ordered (alphabetical by `id` unless documented otherwise).

### When to use JSON

Only when data is generated by external tools, loaded dynamically at runtime, or edited by non-programmers. Always validate JSON against the TypeScript interface at load time (Zod or manual type guard). Never trust raw JSON with `as SomeType`.

### Never use for game data

YAML, TOML, XML, CSV, plain text, or `.js` files.

---

## Constants & Tuning

All balance and behavior numbers live in `src/game/data/constants.ts`.

- `UPPER_SNAKE_CASE` for all exported constants.
- Always comment the unit: pixels, seconds, ms, percent, frames.
- Group related constants under section comments.
- No magic numbers in `engine/` code — extract every literal into a named constant.

```typescript
export const PLAYER_SPEED = 200;         // pixels/sec
export const BASE_CRIT_CHANCE = 0.05;    // 5%
export const RESPAWN_DELAY = 2000;       // ms
```

---

## Asset Formats

| Category | Preferred | Avoid |
|----------|-----------|-------|
| Sprites / images | `.png` | `.jpg` (lossy artifacts on sprites) |
| Audio — SFX | `.ogg`, `.wav` | `.mp3` (licensing, worse quality-per-byte) |
| Audio — Music | `.ogg` | `.mp3` |
| Fonts | `.woff2` | `.ttf`, `.otf` (larger) |
| Level / map data | `.json` | Unstructured text |
| Spritesheets | `.png` + `.json` atlas | — |

- No spaces in asset filenames. `kebab-case` only.
- Always handle asset load failure at runtime — see error handling in copilot instructions.

---

## Quick Location Reference

| What | Where |
|------|-------|
| Data type interfaces | `data/types.ts` |
| Static game definitions | `data/*.ts` |
| Tuning values | `data/constants.ts` |
| Level data from tools | `assets/maps/*.json` |
| Sprites, audio, fonts | `assets/` |
| React UI | `ui/` |
| Game engine logic | `engine/` |
| Pure utilities | `utils/` |
| CSS variables & base styles | `styles/` |
